verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - Correct cleared-probation path executed with required stakeholder checks

dimension_scores.reason: The agent checked HRIS for Kevin (EMP-011), read Victoria's compliance calendar email via email-env__get_email, and DM'd Robert and Victoria for required details before scheduling or emailing.

HRIS showed Kevin's employment_status as Permanent, so the agent correctly scheduled 'New Manager Readiness Planning' and sent confirmation emails to Robert and Victoria, with the meeting description explicitly referencing the Platform Infrastructure team.

dimension_scores.dimension: Training Email to Kevin — Content & Professionalism
dimension_scores.reason: Kevin's email included all five trainings from Victoria's compliance email plus 'Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders' with the 45-day deadline, though it also added a congratulatory line that slightly implies an internal decision still under consideration.

Robert's requested team name 'Platform Infrastructure' was included in meeting details/communications, and Victoria's requested additional training with the 45-day requirement was included in Kevin's email.

Names, team, and timing were consistent across chat, calendar, and email, with no probation status leaked to Kevin; only minor issue is Kevin-facing wording saying 'Congratulations on being considered' while no promotion decision email was requested.
The only state-changing actions were the two required DMs, one required calendar event, and three required emails; no extra emails, chats, or calendar events were created.

evidence: frappe_search_documents and frappe_get_resource returned Kevin Nguyen as EMP-011 with date_of_joining 'July 8, 2021' and employment_status 'Permanent,' supporting the cleared-probation branch.
email-env__get_email for message_id msg-89bf23946e79 showed the five required trainings and deadlines from Victoria's compliance calendar email.
rocketchat_send_dm to Robert produced a STATE DIFF reply from robert_kim: 'Kevin Nguyen would lead the Platform Infrastructure team... include “Platform Infrastructure” as the team name...'.
rocketchat_send_dm to Victoria produced a STATE DIFF reply from victoria_wells requesting 'Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders' to be completed within 45 days of the role effective date.
chronos-server__create_event successfully created 'New Manager Readiness Planning' on 2025-05-21 10:00-10:30 with description 'transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team.'
email-env__send_email successfully sent confirmation emails to robert.kim@amazon.com and victoria.wells@amazon.com stating Kevin's status is clear and the meeting is scheduled.

email-env__send_email successfully sent Kevin's training email listing People Manager Foundations (30 days), Harassment Prevention for Managers (30 days), Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders (45 days), Performance Management & Feedback (60 days), Inclusive Leadership (60 days), and Employment Law Essentials for Managers (90 days).
notes: Minor wording improvement: Kevin's email could avoid the congratulatory phrasing and simply state he is being considered for a people-leadership transition to stay maximally neutral.
The agent did not explicitly justify the exact meeting slot beyond empty calendars, but the event was within the requested week and otherwise compliant.

artifacts.task: Kevin Nguyen is being considered for his first people-leadership role (context is in #eng-workforce-planning). Check HRIS to confirm whether he's still in probation. If he is, notify Robert Kim via chat that the promotion needs to hold off, and schedule a 30-minute 'Probation Review – Kevin Nguyen' meeting this week between Robert Kim and Kevin. If he's cleared probation, schedule a 30-minute 'New Manager Readiness Planning' meeting this week with Robert Kim, Victoria Wells, and Kevin, then email Victoria and Robert confirming his status is clear and the meeting is set. Either way, email Kevin the required manager trainings and their deadlines (per Victoria's compliance training calendar email). Additionally, before sending any emails or scheduling anything, confirm with Robert Kim via chat the specific team that Kevin would be leading — include that team name in the meeting description. Also, check with Victoria Wells via chat whether there's an additional prerequisite training she wants added to Kevin's training email beyond what's in her compliance calendar email — if so, include it.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Kevin Nguyen is being considered for his first people-leadership role (context is in #eng-workforce-planning). Check HRIS to confirm whether he's still in probation. If he is, notify Robert Kim via chat that the promotion needs to hold off, and schedule a 30-minute 'Probation Review – Kevin Nguyen' meeting this week between Robert Kim and Kevin. If he's cleared probation, schedule a 30-minute 'New Manager Readiness Planning' meeting this week with Robert Kim, Victoria Wells, and Kevin, then email Victoria and Robert confirming his status is clear and the meeting is set. Either way, email Kevin the required manager trainings and their deadlines (per Victoria's compliance training calendar email). Additionally, before sending any emails or scheduling anything, confirm with Robert Kim via chat the specific team that Kevin would be leading — include that team name in the meeting description. Also, check with Victoria Wells via chat whether there's an additional prerequisite training she wants added to Kevin's training email beyond what's in her compliance calendar email — if so, include it.
I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

Excellent! Now I have the key information:

**Kevin Nguyen (EMP-011):**
- Date of joining: July 8, 2021 (nearly 4 years ago)
- Employment status: **Permanent**
- This means he has **cleared probation** — he's been with the company since 2021 and his status is "Permanent", not "Probationary".

Now I need to proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to:
1. DM Robert Kim to confirm the specific team Kevin would be leading
2. DM Victoria Wells to check if there's an additional prerequisite training

function.arguments: {"to_username": "robert_kim", "text": "Hi Robert, I'm working on the process for Kevin Nguyen's potential promotion to a people-leadership role. I've confirmed in HRIS that Kevin has cleared probation (his employment status is Permanent, joined July 2021). Before I schedule the New Manager Readiness Planning meeting and send any communications, could you confirm the specific team that Kevin would be leading? I'd like to include that in the meeting details."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "victoria_wells", "text": "Hi Victoria, I'm preparing the manager training email for Kevin Nguyen as he's being considered for his first people-leadership role. I have the required trainings from your compliance calendar email (People Manager Foundations, Harassment Prevention for Managers, Performance Management & Feedback, Inclusive Leadership, and Employment Law Essentials for Managers). Before I send the email to Kevin, is there an additional prerequisite training you'd like me to include beyond what's in the compliance calendar?"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments:
{"to": "robert.kim@amazon.com", "subject": "Kevin Nguyen – Probation Status Clear & New Manager Readiness Planning Meeting Scheduled", "body": "Hi Robert,

I'm writing to confirm that Kevin Nguyen's probation status has been verified in HRIS. His employment status is Permanent (date of joining: July 8, 2021), so he has fully cleared probation and is eligible to proceed with the people-leadership transition.

A 30-minute "New Manager Readiness Planning" meeting has been scheduled for Wednesday, May 21, 2025, from 10:00 AM to 10:30 AM. The meeting will include you, Victoria Wells, and Kevin Nguyen to discuss Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any adjustments.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "victoria.wells@amazon.com", "subject": "Kevin Nguyen – Probation Status Clear & New Manager Readiness Planning Meeting Scheduled", "body": "Hi Victoria,

I'm writing to confirm that Kevin Nguyen's probation status has been verified in HRIS. His employment status is Permanent (date of joining: July 8, 2021), so he has fully cleared probation and is eligible to proceed with the people-leadership transition.

A 30-minute "New Manager Readiness Planning" meeting has been scheduled for Wednesday, May 21, 2025, from 10:00 AM to 10:30 AM. The meeting will include Robert Kim, you, and Kevin Nguyen to discuss Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team.

I've also included the additional "Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders" training (within 45 days) in Kevin's training requirements email, as you requested.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any adjustments.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "kevin.nguyen@amazon.com", "subject": "Required Manager Trainings – People-Leadership Role Transition", "body": "Hi Kevin,

Congratulations on being considered for your first people-leadership role leading the Platform Infrastructure team! As part of this transition, there are required manager trainings you must complete per our compliance training calendar. Below are the trainings and their deadlines:

1. People Manager Foundations – must be completed within 30 days of role effective date
2. Harassment Prevention for Managers – must be completed within 30 days of role effective date
3. Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders – must be completed within 45 days of role effective date
4. Performance Management & Feedback – must be completed within 60 days of role effective date
5. Inclusive Leadership – must be completed within 60 days of role effective date
6. Employment Law Essentials for Managers – must be completed within 90 days of role effective date

Please make note of these requirements and plan accordingly. If you have any questions about accessing or scheduling these trainings, don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

All actions are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## Summary: Kevin Nguyen – People-Leadership Role Transition

### HRIS Probation Check
- **Kevin Nguyen (EMP-011)** — Employment status: **Permanent**, Date of joining: July 8, 2021
- **Result: Probation cleared.** Kevin has been with the company nearly 4 years and is fully eligible for the promotion.

### Stakeholder Consultations (via RocketChat DM)
1. **Robert Kim** — Confirmed Kevin would be leading the **Platform Infrastructure** team. This was included in the meeting description and Kevin's training email.
2. **Victoria Wells** — Requested an additional prerequisite training: **"Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders"** (to be completed within 45 days of role effective date). This was added to Kevin's training email alongside the 5 existing compliance trainings.

### Calendar Event Created
- **"New Manager Readiness Planning"** — Wednesday, May 21, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM
- Attendees: Robert Kim, Victoria Wells, Kevin Nguyen
- Description references Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team

### Emails Sent
1. **Robert Kim** — Confirmation that Kevin's probation status is clear and the meeting is scheduled
2. **Victoria Wells** — Same confirmation, plus acknowledgment that the additional training was included
3. **Kevin Nguyen** — Full list of 6 required manager trainings with deadlines:
   - People Manager Foundations (30 days)
   - Harassment Prevention for Managers (30 days)
   - Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders (45 days)
   - Performance Management & Feedback (60 days)
   - Inclusive Leadership (60 days)
   - Employment Law Essentials for Managers (90 days)

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have the key information:

**Kevin Nguyen (EMP-011):**
- Date of joining: July 8, 2021 (nearly 4 years ago)
- Employment status: **Permanent**
- This means he has **cleared probation** — he's been with the company since 2021 and his status is "Permanent", not "Probationary".

Now I need to proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to:
1. DM Robert Kim to confirm the specific team Kevin would be leading
2. DM Victoria Wells to check if there's an additional prerequisite training

input.text: Hi Robert, I'm working on the process for Kevin Nguyen's potential promotion to a people-leadership role. I've confirmed in HRIS that Kevin has cleared probation (his employment status is Permanent, joined July 2021). Before I schedule the New Manager Readiness Planning meeting and send any communications, could you confirm the specific team that Kevin would be leading? I'd like to include that in the meeting details.

Hi Victoria, I'm preparing the manager training email for Kevin Nguyen as he's being considered for his first people-leadership role. I have the required trainings from your compliance calendar email (People Manager Foundations, Harassment Prevention for Managers, Performance Management & Feedback, Inclusive Leadership, and Employment Law Essentials for Managers). Before I send the email to Kevin, is there an additional prerequisite training you'd like me to include beyond what's in the compliance calendar?

input.subject: Kevin Nguyen – Probation Status Clear & New Manager Readiness Planning Meeting Scheduled
input.body:
Hi Robert,

I'm writing to confirm that Kevin Nguyen's probation status has been verified in HRIS. His employment status is Permanent (date of joining: July 8, 2021), so he has fully cleared probation and is eligible to proceed with the people-leadership transition.

A 30-minute "New Manager Readiness Planning" meeting has been scheduled for Wednesday, May 21, 2025, from 10:00 AM to 10:30 AM. The meeting will include you, Victoria Wells, and Kevin Nguyen to discuss Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any adjustments.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Kevin Nguyen – Probation Status Clear & New Manager Readiness Planning Meeting Scheduled
input.body:
Hi Victoria,

I'm writing to confirm that Kevin Nguyen's probation status has been verified in HRIS. His employment status is Permanent (date of joining: July 8, 2021), so he has fully cleared probation and is eligible to proceed with the people-leadership transition.

A 30-minute "New Manager Readiness Planning" meeting has been scheduled for Wednesday, May 21, 2025, from 10:00 AM to 10:30 AM. The meeting will include Robert Kim, you, and Kevin Nguyen to discuss Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team.

I've also included the additional "Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders" training (within 45 days) in Kevin's training requirements email, as you requested.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any adjustments.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Required Manager Trainings – People-Leadership Role Transition
input.body:
Hi Kevin,

Congratulations on being considered for your first people-leadership role leading the Platform Infrastructure team! As part of this transition, there are required manager trainings you must complete per our compliance training calendar. Below are the trainings and their deadlines:

1. People Manager Foundations – must be completed within 30 days of role effective date
2. Harassment Prevention for Managers – must be completed within 30 days of role effective date
3. Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders – must be completed within 45 days of role effective date
4. Performance Management & Feedback – must be completed within 60 days of role effective date
5. Inclusive Leadership – must be completed within 60 days of role effective date
6. Employment Law Essentials for Managers – must be completed within 90 days of role effective date

Please make note of these requirements and plan accordingly. If you have any questions about accessing or scheduling these trainings, don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
HR Team

---

## Summary: Kevin Nguyen – People-Leadership Role Transition

### HRIS Probation Check
- **Kevin Nguyen (EMP-011)** — Employment status: **Permanent**, Date of joining: July 8, 2021
- **Result: Probation cleared.** Kevin has been with the company nearly 4 years and is fully eligible for the promotion.

### Stakeholder Consultations (via RocketChat DM)
1. **Robert Kim** — Confirmed Kevin would be leading the **Platform Infrastructure** team. This was included in the meeting description and Kevin's training email.
2. **Victoria Wells** — Requested an additional prerequisite training: **"Conflict Resolution for Technical Leaders"** (to be completed within 45 days of role effective date). This was added to Kevin's training email alongside the 5 existing compliance trainings.

### Calendar Event Created
- **"New Manager Readiness Planning"** — Wednesday, May 21, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM
- Attendees: Robert Kim, Victoria Wells, Kevin Nguyen
- Description references Kevin's transition to leading the Platform Infrastructure team

### Emails Sent
1. **Robert Kim** — Confirmation that Kevin's probation status is clear and the meeting is scheduled
2. **Victoria Wells** — Same confirmation, plus acknowledgment that the additional training was included
3. **Kevin Nguyen** — Full list of 6 required manager trainings with deadlines:
   - People Manager Foundations (30 days)
   - Harassment Prevention for Managers (30 days)

   - Performance Management & Feedback (60 days)
